document.title: Usability Engineering Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability engineering report for HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports usability engineering, risk control, verification, validation, and accompanying documentation for the detector.
keyPoints: The report identifies intended purpose, patient population, intended users, conditions of use, frequently used functions, hazards, use errors, and risk controls.
The detector is indicated for general radiographic digital imaging of human anatomy and is not to be used for mammography.
Normative references include EN ISO 14971:2019 and EN 62366-1:2015.
Usability validation used five intended users and reported all users accepted in the green zone.

concepts.name: Usability Engineering Report
concepts.evidence: This document is the Usability Engineering Report for X-ray detector.

concepts.name: Intended Purpose
concepts.evidence: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

concepts.name: Risk Control
concepts.evidence: There are three options for risk control options. To reduce risk using identified risk control methods, manufacturers should use one or more risk control options.

concepts.name: Usability Validation
concepts.evidence: Tests were conducted according to the test methods established in the usability validation plan, and all users were successful in the acceptable range of the test criteria.

concepts.name: Primary Operating Functions
concepts.evidence: For the primary actuation function of this product, see Section 5.4 PRIMARY OPERATING FUNCTIONS.

evidence.claim: The document is Report No. A-UER-001 Rev.5.
The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Dec 01, 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared Quality team Han Jimin Dec 01, 2022 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin Dec 01, 2022 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Dec 01, 2022

evidence.claim: Revision 5 was issued on Dec 01, 2022 according to the update of applicable standards.
evidence.evidenceText: 5 Dec 01, 2022 Revision according to the update of applicable standards.

evidence.claim: The report applies to HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

evidence.claim: The report is a usability engineering report for an X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This document is the Usability Engineering Report for X-ray detector.

evidence.claim: Normative references are EN ISO 14971:2019 and EN 62366-1:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019, Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices; EN 62366-1:2015 - Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic systems for human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The detector is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: The detector is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended user is a medical professional with a professional license who is familiar with the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: A medical professional with a professional license; Knowledge: Conditions that satisfy both of the following two conditions - A medical professional with a professional license - familiar with user manual

evidence.claim: Operating conditions are +5 to 40 degrees Celsius and 30 to 75 percent relative humidity without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:+5 ~ 40℃ - Relative humidity:30 ~ 75 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: Storage conditions are -10 to +50 degrees Celsius and 10 to 80 percent relative humidity without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:-10 ~ + 50℃ - Relative humidity:10 ~ 80 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: The document states an expected service life of 5 years.
The device is classified as not applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.1.5 Classification of APPLIED PARTS Not applied part

evidence.claim: Frequently used functions include user manual familiarization and connection of power, data, LAN, trigger, and communication cables.
evidence.evidenceText: Frequently Used functions: Familiarize yourself with the user manual before and after the product; Connecting the power cord to the power supply; Connecting the Data Cable to the Power Supply and the Detector; Connecting the LAN cable to the power supply; Trigger cable connection to power supply; LAN cable and PC main body connection; Connecting the communication cable to the power supply

evidence.claim: Known or foreseeable usability-related hazards include wet-hand power cord handling, wet-hand cable handling, contamination, hard pulling of connected cable, and wet-hand power cord removal.
evidence.evidenceText: Before use Mounting the power cord with wet hands; Mounting cables with wet hands; Operation of the main power button with wet; During use Status of contamination on the product surface; When the connected cable of the detector is pulled hard; After use Remove the power cord with wet hands

evidence.claim: Usability validation planned five typical intended users.
The usability validation reported all users successful and accepted as green zones.
evidence.evidenceText: Tests were conducted according to the test methods established in the usability validation plan, and all users were successful in the acceptable range of the test criteria. Therefore, all users were accepted as green zones.

evidence.claim: Accompanying documents listed are Usability Engineering Plan A-UEP-001, Risk management report A-RMR-0-01, and TCF TCF-001.
evidence.evidenceText: Accompanying document Usability Engineering Plan (A-UEP-001) Risk management report (A-RMR-0-01) TCF (TCF-001)

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the source text; source metadata has authoritySignal FDA while the source path indicates CE MDD and the text cites EN standards.

Conflicting product evidence appears in section 6.11: the purpose states a device that applies heat to relieve muscle pain, and validation text mentions medical warmer BPD-1700 G, while the rest of the document concerns HAD1717MC/HAD1717MG X-ray detectors.
Source text contains apparent OCR or translation quality issues, including inconsistent wording such as HAD17MC, IEC62336, data code, and repeated or malformed section numbering.
